name: Low-Fat Banoffi Pie
recipeIngredient: 8 oz Cereal - I tried to use Golden Grahams, but could only buy a one-serving pack, which wasn't quite enough. Added some All-Bran. Another time, Shreddies might be used instead.
American Readers may prefer non-fat graham crackers (which are unavailable in my area).
1 tbsp Pear and Apple spread (I used Pear and Apricot; apple butter would be a good substitute)
1 Tin Skimmed Condensed Milk - if unavailable, use the thinnest low-fat milk possible
About 8 oz Cream Substitute (I mixed Fromage Frais with Quark; yogurt or another cream substitute can also be used)
recipeInstructions:
1. The day before, boil an unopened tin of skimmed condensed milk for three hours—do not let it boil dry. Allow to cool.
2. Make the biscuit base by crushing the cereal into crumbs, melting the fruit spread in the microwave (about 30 seconds), mixing them together, and pressing into the flan dish. Leave in the fridge to harden.
3. On the next day, open the can of milk (it will have caramelized). Spread it over the biscuit base; it may stick, so gently swirl it around.
4. Top with sliced bananas and instantly spread the cream substitute (watch for browning).
5. Refrigerate until ready to serve.
